name: Crystal Sickness
tagline: Golbez drain off a high-power creature (needs digging + cycles)
idea: The deck floods the board with cheap and free artifacts, uses Golbez's surveil triggers to fill the graveyard and sculpt draws, then exploits the end-step ability to recur high-power creatures and drain opponents.

briefKills.line: Phyrexian Dreadnought in the yard (12 power) recur + re-bin for 1 mana each turn → 12 drain to each opponent every end step → ~4 cycles
Master of Etherium in the yard (= your artifact count; reads in graveyard) → 10+ drain to each opponent, scales with the 8-artifact board

openings: Weakest axis — Kill reliability (4/5): the judges' soft spot.
Dev-gated (8 artifacts + fat creature), not pure finding.
Decap never-closes in 20% of goldfish games — a grind risk.
Tables (all three) never-close 34% of the time.

clock.caption: Decap (one opponent) lands around T11; the full table around T13.

gamePlan: The deck floods the board with cheap and free artifacts, uses Golbez's surveil triggers to fill the graveyard and sculpt draws, then exploits the end-step ability to recur high-power creatures and drain opponents. Every artifact entering the battlefield is simultaneously card selection (surveil), engine fuel (artifact count), and kill setup (8+ threshold).
winLine: Golbez drain off a high-power creature (needs digging + cycles)

finishers.name: Golbez Drain with High-Power Creatures
finishers.note: With 8+ artifacts, Golbez's end-step ability returns a creature card from graveyard to hand AND each opponent loses life equal to that card's power.

finishers.name: Tezzeret, Master of the Bridge
finishers.note: Tezzeret, Master of the Bridge is {4}{U}{B} (often discounted by his own static "Creature and planeswalker spells you cast have affinity for artifacts").

finishers.name: Mirrodin Besieged Phyrexian Win Condition
finishers.note: Choosing Phyrexian on entry: at the beginning of your end step, you draw a card then discard a card; **then if there are 15 or more artifact cards in *your* graveyard, target opponent loses the game.** This is a self-mill/self-loot alt-win, not opponent-mill.

finishers.name: Thopter Army Combat
finishers.note: With Sai, Efficient Construction, Stridehangar Automaton, and The Mechanist generating Thopters over multiple turns, a board of 6+ flying Thopters threatens lethal in the air.

finishers.name: Urza Construct + Artifact Mana Dominance
finishers.note: Urza creates a Construct token with power/toughness equal to your artifact count.

finishers.name: Tezzeret, Cruel Captain Emblem
finishers.note: Tezzeret, Cruel Captain gains loyalty on each artifact ETB (often 3–5 per turn cycle).

rulings.name: Golbez's 4-artifact threshold is checked at trigger AND resolution
rulings.note: drop below 4 in between and the whole ability fizzles. The 8-artifact threshold is checked only at resolution. If there's no legal creature target in your yard, the entire ability fails — no drain even at 8+.

rulings.name: Master of Etherium's power = your artifact count, and this works in the graveyard
rulings.note: it's the best drain target because it scales with the same board that turns on the 8+ threshold.

rulings.name: Every artifact token generator triggers on CAST, not ETB.
rulings.note: Token copies, reanimation, and cheated-in artifacts do NOT make tokens — plan to cast from hand.

rulings.name: Mirrodin Besieged Phyrexian mode is a self-loot + self-mill alt-win
rulings.note: (15 artifacts in your yard), not opponent-mill. Mirran mode (Myr per artifact cast) is the combat-plan alternative — choose at the table.

rulings.name: Fomori Vault is NOT an artifact land
Flare of Denial's alt-cost needs a nontoken blue creature; Flare of Malice needs a nontoken black one
rulings.note: (the deck has few). Plan free-cast turns around what you can actually sacrifice.

keep.mixed: dev-gated (8 artifacts + fat creature), not pure finding

mull.strategy: Looking for: lands + 2–3 cheap artifacts + a token generator or cost reducer. Golbez costs only {U}{B}, so he lands T2 routinely. Cheap payoffs without fuel are the trap keep: Dreadnought or Tezzeret on curve-perfect lands does nothing without 2–3 cheap artifacts behind it — artifact DENSITY is the real gate, and no single "early play" substitutes for it.

killTree.title: Crystal Sickness — Golbez, Crystal Collector
killTree.root:
Golbez online with 8+ artifacts
each end step return a creature from your yard; with 8+ artifacts EACH opponent loses life = its power
killTree.stall:
deploy artifacts toward the 8-count and bin a drain bomb (Dreadnought self-sac / Troll swampcycle / surveil);
refill the hand — the engine is card draw, not the curve
killTree.caption: cheapest line first · fall to the next

lines.need:
Phyrexian Dreadnought in the yard (12 power)
recur + re-bin for 1 mana each turn
lines.kill: 12 drain to each opponent every end step → ~4 cycles

lines.need:
Master of Etherium in the yard
(= your artifact count; reads in graveyard)
lines.kill: 10+ drain to each opponent, scales with the 8-artifact board

lines.need:
Tezzeret, Master of the Bridge +2
(X = artifacts, to EACH opponent, once/turn)
lines.kill: stacks with Golbez → 20+ life loss per opp per turn

lines.need:
Urza's Construct (= artifacts) / 6+ flying Thopters
(3/3 with Master + Stridehangar)
lines.kill: focus-fire one opponent in the air (decap leads by ~2)

lines.need:
Mirrodin Besieged (Phyrexian)
+ 15 artifact cards in YOUR graveyard (fallback)
lines.kill: target opponent loses the game each end step (dodges lifegain/fog)
